title: rubus flagellaris
intro: Rubus flagellaris, also known as the thimbleberry, is a deciduous shrub that belongs to the rose family. It has medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, leaves, and stems, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rubus flagellaris include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as promote wound healing and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Rubus flagellaris can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for topical application.
